folders.title: Preprocessing
folders.blurb: Turns raw F-SAR products into the beamformed Capon tomogram, flattened interferometric stack, per-pixel geometry field and stack-overview plots that seed the dataset and training pipelines.

diagrams.title: Stack Generation
diagrams.blurb: The main preprocessing run ingests raw F-SAR products through PyRat, beamforms a Capon tomogram, builds the flattened interferometric stack, derives the per-pixel geometry field, and records an artifact manifest for the dataset stage.

nodes.label: pre_process.py
nodes.fn: Bootstraps the pinned env and builds one ProcessingConfig session per win filter, then hands them to PreProcessScheduler.

nodes.label: ProcessingConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass carrying crop, track selection, polarisation, beamforming method and run path settings.

nodes.label: ProcessingPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences the tomogram, interferometric-inputs and geometry-field stages, then triggers the run manifest write.

nodes.label: ArtifactRegistry
nodes.fn: Creates the run directory structure and resolves the canonical .npy artifact output paths by name.

nodes.label: TomogramLauncher
nodes.fn: Serialises a tomogram job spec and launches the pyrat-env subprocess (generate_tomogram.py).

nodes.label: InterferogramLauncher
nodes.fn: Serialises an interferogram job spec, launches the pyrat-env subprocess (generate_interferograms.py) and reads back stack shapes and pass labels.

nodes.label: TomogramGenerator
nodes.fn: Runs in the pyrat env; dispatches PyRat Capon jobs over azimuth subsections via TomogramProcessor and concatenates them into the tomogram cube and DEM.

nodes.label: InterferogramGenerator
nodes.fn: Runs in the pyrat env; loads primary/secondary SLCs through PyRat, flattens interferograms by DEM-phase deramping, and extracts track baselines, profiles and parameters.

nodes.label: GeometryFieldBuilder
nodes.fn: Builds the per-pixel kz geometry field for the physics loss from track parameters and per-azimuth baseline profiles.

nodes.label: MetadataManager
nodes.fn: Writes the config snapshot, per-stage metadata files and the dataset.json layout manifest.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Beamformed Capon tomogram cube (height x azimuth x range) consumed by the dataset stage.

artifacts.name: dem_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Full-stack DEM height map over the crop.

artifacts.name: primary/secondaries/interferograms.npy
artifacts.desc: Primary SLC, secondary SLC stack and DEM-flattened interferometric stack.

artifacts.name: geometry_field.npz
artifacts.desc: Per-pixel kz geometry field wired into the physics loss.

artifacts.name: track_parameters.json / track_profiles.npz
artifacts.desc: Per-track acquisition parameters and per-azimuth baseline profiles.

artifacts.name: dataset.json
artifacts.desc: Stack layout manifest: artifact filenames, crop, max_amplitude_clip and pass_labels.

artifacts.name: tomogram_spec.json / interferogram_spec.json
artifacts.desc: Job spec handed to the pyrat-env subprocess.

diagrams.title: Stack Overview Plots
diagrams.blurb: A decoupled analysis pass that reads each preprocessing run's dataset.json manifest and renders SLC amplitude, interferogram amplitude/phase and DEM overview figures.

nodes.label: analyze_preprocessing.py
nodes.fn: Bootstraps the env and runs PreprocessingInferenceScheduler over the selected preprocessing trials.

nodes.label: PreprocessInferenceConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass naming the runs directory and the specific run tags (or auto-discovery) to plot.

nodes.label: StackInferenceTrialCollector
nodes.fn: Discovers preprocessing run directories that contain a data/dataset.json manifest.

nodes.label: PreprocessingInferenceScheduler
nodes.fn: Sequences one StackInference session per discovered trial run directory.

nodes.label: StackInferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Reads a run's dataset.json layout and drives the plotter over its stack artifacts.

nodes.label: StackPlotter
nodes.fn: Memory-maps the primary/secondary SLCs, interferograms and DEM and renders amplitude, phase and height overview figures.

artifacts.name: dataset.json
artifacts.desc: Stack layout manifest read to discover trials and locate stack artifacts.

artifacts.name: primary/secondaries/interferograms.npy
artifacts.desc: SLC and interferometric stacks memory-mapped for plotting.

artifacts.name: dem_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Full-stack DEM height map memory-mapped for plotting.

artifacts.name: stack overview figures
artifacts.desc: SLC amplitude, interferogram amplitude/phase and DEM figures under the run's images/ tree.

folders.title: Parameter Extraction
folders.blurb: The parameter-extraction subsystem fits per-pixel multi-Gaussian height profiles to reduced tomograms on the GPU and re-analyses the fitted parameter stacks into fit-quality metrics and diagnostic plots.

diagrams.title: Per-pixel K-Gaussian mixture fit
diagrams.blurb: Fits a per-pixel K-Gaussian mixture to every tomogram profile while sweeping K, lambda and fit modes, and writes the mu-sorted parameter stack with fit diagnostics and run metadata.

nodes.label: extract_params
nodes.fn: Bootstraps the sweep: pins GPUs, resolves the dataset queue and per-permutation plans, then runs one ParamExtractionPipeline per plan with a shared PeakInitialiser.

nodes.label: ExtractParamsEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass holding the dataset base path, sweep lists (fit_k_values, fit_lambda_values, fit_modes), height range and Adam/batch knobs for each extraction run.

nodes.label: tomogram_full.npy
nodes.fn: Full tomogram cube (H x Az x R) produced by the tomogram-generation pipeline, discovered from each dataset's data/ directory.

nodes.label: Queue + Plan Resolvers
nodes.fn: DatasetQueueResolver lists valid dataset directories under the base path and ExtractionPlanResolver expands the K x lambda x fit-mode grid into a list of ExtractionConfig plans.

nodes.label: ParamExtractionPipeline
nodes.fn: Per-plan orchestrator that discovers the tomogram and height range, drives the fit, mu-sorts the returned Gaussian stack via ParameterExtractor, and hands arrays to IO.

nodes.label: SigmaFittingExtractor
nodes.fn: Streams range-bin batches and sequences the three-phase fit (CPU peak init, GPU Adam scan, best-K selection), accumulating the parameter and diagnostic maps.

nodes.label: PeakInitialiser
nodes.fn: CPU prominence peak-finder (scipy find_peaks over a process pool) that seeds per-pixel amplitude, mean and sigma initial guesses shared across all K.

nodes.label: SigmaAdamKernel
nodes.fn: JAX jit/pmap Adam scan (selected by KernelBackendSelector) that fits per-pixel K-Gaussian amplitudes, means and sigmas, masking amp/mu gradients by fit mode.

nodes.label: BestKSelector
nodes.fn: Scores each K by normalized MSE plus a lambda*K complexity penalty and picks the penalised-MSE-minimising model order per pixel, emitting best params and mse/penalised maps.

nodes.label: ParameterIO + MetadataManager
nodes.fn: Saves the mu-sorted parameters.npy, the fit_diagnostics.npz and the param_extraction_meta.json describing K, lambda, thresholds and the source tomogram.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Reduced tomogram whose per-pixel amplitude profiles are fitted; mmap-loaded range-batch by range-batch.

artifacts.name: parameters.npy
artifacts.fmt: npy - (3*k_max, Az, R) float32, mu-sorted per pixel
artifacts.desc: Per-pixel Gaussian parameters (amp, mu, sigma per slot), sorted by mu among active slots; the backbone regression target.

artifacts.name: fit_diagnostics.npz
artifacts.desc: Model-order-selection diagnostics kept for metric recompute and ambiguity plots.

artifacts.name: param_extraction_meta.json
artifacts.fmt: json - k_max, lambda_k, height_range, thresholds, source_tomogram
artifacts.desc: Self-describing run metadata; k_max drives downstream n_gaussians and preprocessing/threshold reuse.

diagrams.title: Fit-metric recompute and plotting
diagrams.blurb: Reloads an extraction trial to recompute fit-quality metrics and summaries and to render the spatial, distribution, metrics and example-fit plot families.

nodes.label: analyze_param_extraction
nodes.fn: Thin analysis entry that pins threads and runs the inference scheduler over one or more selected extraction trials.

nodes.label: ParamExtractionInferenceConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass giving the params directory, optional run_tags selecting trials, and the make_plots flag.

nodes.label: extraction run dir
nodes.fn: An extraction run directory (parameters.npy, fit_diagnostics.npz, param_extraction_meta.json and the source tomogram) produced by the Extraction stage.

nodes.label: InferenceScheduler + TrialCollector
nodes.fn: Collects trial directories by rglob of param_extraction_meta.json (or explicit run_tags) and runs one ParamRunInferencePipeline session per trial sequentially.

nodes.label: ParamRunInferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Per-trial orchestrator that loads arrays, recomputes fit metrics, writes fit_metrics_summary.json, and optionally renders the plot family.

nodes.label: ParameterIO
nodes.fn: Loads parameters.npy, fit_diagnostics.npz and param_extraction_meta.json from the run directory for the recompute.

nodes.label: FittingMetricsCalculator
nodes.fn: Reloads the tomogram and recomputes per-pixel R2, active-Gaussian count, peak-to-floor contrast and K-selection-margin maps plus global/per-K/contrast summaries.

nodes.label: FittingResultPlotter family
nodes.fn: FittingResultPlotter drives the spatial-map, distribution, metrics-bar and example-fit leaf plotters to write publication-quality figure families under images/.

artifacts.name: parameters.npy
artifacts.desc: The fitted Gaussian parameter stack reloaded for metric recompute and plotting.

artifacts.name: fit_diagnostics.npz
artifacts.desc: Selection diagnostics feeding the K-margin/ambiguity metrics and plots.

artifacts.name: param_extraction_meta.json
artifacts.fmt: json - k_max, lambda_k, height_range, thresholds, source_tomogram
artifacts.desc: Drives trial discovery and supplies k_max, thresholds, height range and the tomogram path for recompute.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Source tomogram reloaded to compute per-pixel R2 and peak-to-floor contrast against the fits.

artifacts.name: fit_metrics_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Recomputed fit-quality summary surfaced in the web UI and comparison views.

artifacts.name: result plots (PNG)
artifacts.desc: Spatial colormaps, R2/parameter distributions, metrics bars and example fits written under the run's images/ directory.

folders.title: Backbone
folders.blurb: The backbone subsystem turns a preprocessed F-SAR stack into per-pixel Gaussian elevation-profile predictions: it assembles normalized patch DataLoaders, trains a CNN backbone under a curriculum of curve/parameter/physics losses, and runs stitched-cube inference with metrics, a classical Capon baseline and a full report.

diagrams.title: Patch dataset assembly and normalization
diagrams.blurb: Crops the preprocessed stack into per-split patch datasets, fits per-channel normalization on the train split, and emits normalized train/val/test DataLoaders plus the dataset and stats metadata consumed by training and inference.

nodes.label: DatasetConfig
nodes.fn: Declares split regions, input/output channel selection, patch size/stride, secondary labels and normalization settings for the dataset build.

nodes.label: GeometryField (kz)
nodes.fn: Per-pixel kz geometry field produced by preprocessing, loaded and sliced per split region when a physics loss is active.

nodes.label: DatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: Builds the train split, fits stats on it, then builds val/test with that normalizer and writes crop/patch/config metadata before returning loaders.

nodes.label: Cropper / Layout
nodes.fn: Memmaps the preprocessing primary/secondaries/interferograms/parameters/DEM arrays and slices each split region into a stacked input tensor and targets.

nodes.label: Patcher
nodes.fn: Computes the padded patch grid from spatial size, patch size and stride and extracts each windowed patch on demand.

nodes.label: PatchDataset / MultiRegionDataset
nodes.fn: Assembles the per-patch input tensor from configured channel representations, selects GT parameter channels, and normalizes both on access.

nodes.label: SpatialAugmenter
nodes.fn: Applies random flips, rot90 and normalized-space noise to train patches, keeping the kz field co-transformed.

nodes.label: StatsComputer
nodes.fn: Fits per-slot-kind input and per-role output loc/scale strategies over sampled train values and saves them as normalization stats.

nodes.label: Normalizer
nodes.fn: Applies and inverts per-channel log1p/z-score normalization on input and output tensors using the fitted Stats.

nodes.label: MetadataWriter
nodes.fn: Serializes the dataset config, global crop, per-split regions and patch grids to JSON in the run meta directory.

nodes.label: Loader
nodes.fn: Wraps the three PatchDatasets in seeded, batched train/val/test DataLoaders handed to the trainer.

artifacts.name: preprocessing arrays (*.npy)
artifacts.desc: Primary, secondaries, interferograms, parameters, DEM and full-tomogram arrays sliced per split region.

artifacts.name: meta/geometry_field.npz
artifacts.desc: Per-pixel kz geometry field subset to the selected secondaries for the physics loss.

artifacts.name: normalization_stats.json
artifacts.desc: Fitted per-channel input/output loc, scale, strategies and output clamp used to (de)normalize everywhere.

artifacts.name: meta/dataset_creation_config.json
artifacts.desc: Full dataset config (splits, patch, input/output config, secondary labels) that inference reconstructs the dataset from.

artifacts.name: meta/crop.json
artifacts.desc: Global crop and per-split region rectangles.

artifacts.name: meta/patch.json
artifacts.desc: Per-split patch grid geometry (counts, padding, stride).

artifacts.name: train/val/test DataLoaders
artifacts.desc: Normalized, batched patch loaders plus the underlying datasets carrying the fitted normalizer.

diagrams.title: Backbone training loop and curriculum losses
diagrams.blurb: Sequences dataset construction, model build, an overfit sanity gate and optional loss-scale probe, then runs the training loop under a curriculum of curve, parameter and physics losses, persisting the best checkpoint, configs and model docs.

nodes.label: train_backbone.py
nodes.fn: Thin bootstrap that pins the GPU and hands off to BackboneTrainingLauncher.

nodes.label: BackboneEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Resolved CLI config carrying backbone name, trainer/dataset/geometry/curriculum settings, overfit-check and probe knobs, and trial-sweep fields.

nodes.label: TrainingLauncher / TrainScheduler / SingleTrainRunner
nodes.fn: Routes to a single seeded run or fans out curriculum/warmup/presence/physics/pair/secondary/patch/input/ablation trial planners across GPUs.

nodes.label: DatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: Dataset-stage boundary that returns train/val/test loaders, the fitted normalizer and the datasets.

nodes.label: TrainingPipeline
nodes.fn: Runs the dataset pipeline, builds the model, runs the overfit gate, then constructs the trainer and drives train plus test.

nodes.label: ModelBuilder / get_backbone
nodes.fn: Instantiates the named backbone with in/out channels and image-size overrides and reports parameter counts.

nodes.label: Trainer
nodes.fn: Runs the epoch loop with EMA, warmup/LR schedule, curriculum loss swap and checkpointing, saving the best model on validation.

nodes.label: Loss (loss_terms catalog)
nodes.fn: Reconstructs Gaussian curves and computes the weighted sum of curve, parameter, TV and physics terms declared in loss_terms.py, plus occupancy diagnostics.

nodes.label: OverfitCheck
nodes.fn: Pre-training sanity gate that memorizes a few sanitized examples and aborts the run on failure.

nodes.label: LossScaleProbe
nodes.fn: Optionally averages each loss term at weight 1 over a few batches to suggest comparable-magnitude weights, then may exit.

nodes.label: ReconstructionFigures / ParamSampler
nodes.fn: Selects representative validation pixels and logs predicted-vs-GT profile reconstructions and predicted-parameter histograms per epoch.

nodes.label: TrainingDocs / ModelInspector
nodes.fn: Runs a shape-hooked forward pass to render a layer-by-layer model documentation table to markdown.

nodes.label: TrainingRunMetadata
nodes.fn: Owns the run directory and logger and persists trainer/model configs, the run summary and final test metrics.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Best-validation state dict plus x_axis, epoch and best-loss metadata loaded for inference.

artifacts.name: checkpoint.pt
artifacts.desc: Periodic/last checkpoint for resume, including optimizer and curriculum state.

artifacts.name: docs/trainer_config.json
artifacts.desc: Resolved trainer config; inference reads the height-axis convention from it.

artifacts.name: meta/model_config.json
artifacts.desc: Persisted backbone model config rebuilt at inference to reconstruct the network.

artifacts.name: meta/run_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Model name, in/out channels, x-axis length, n_gaussians and seed used to rebuild the run.

artifacts.name: docs/model_doc.md
artifacts.desc: Layer-by-layer parameter and shape documentation of the built model.

artifacts.name: meta/overfit_report.json
artifacts.desc: Overfit sanity-gate record; the run aborts if the model cannot memorize the sample.

artifacts.name: test_metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Final held-out test metrics saved at the end of the run.

diagrams.title: Stitched-cube inference, metrics and report
diagrams.blurb: Loads a trained checkpoint and its split, predicts and stitches the elevation-profile cube, scores it against the GT tomogram and a classical Capon baseline, checks interferometric consistency, and renders figures, animations and a markdown report.

nodes.label: infer_backbone.py
nodes.fn: Thin bootstrap that constructs the shared InferenceEntry with RunType.BACKBONE.

nodes.label: InferenceConfig
nodes.fn: Selects run directory, split, checkpoint, stitching window, and toggles for cubes, reduced baseline, data consistency, plots and animations.

nodes.label: InferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Loads the run, predicts the cube, evaluates metrics/reduced/consistency, composes figures and assembles the report.

nodes.label: InferenceMetadata
nodes.fn: Resolves and creates the timestamped output directory tree (figures, animations, logs, cubes, metrics, report paths).

nodes.label: RunLoader
nodes.fn: Rebuilds dataset config, model and single-region PatchDataset, loads the checkpoint and stats and track baselines into a Run.

nodes.label: ModelWrapper
nodes.fn: Wraps the eval network to run a no-grad forward pass and denormalize plus Gaussian-clamp the predicted parameters.

nodes.label: Predictor / CubeStitcher
nodes.fn: Runs the GPU forward pass, reconstructs curves and sorts params on CPU workers, and Hann/centrality-stitches patches into full pred/GT cubes.

nodes.label: ReducedTomogramSynthesizer
nodes.fn: Reproduces a classical Capon tomogram from the trained secondary subset via TomogramLauncher and caches it as the baseline.

nodes.label: Metrics
nodes.fn: Computes curve, per-pixel, SSIM, per-elevation, matched-Gaussian and slot-organization metrics plus the reduced-vs-GT comparison.

nodes.label: DataConsistencyEvaluator
nodes.fn: Reprojects pred/GT curves to track coherences via the kz field and scores them against the measured interferogram phase.

nodes.label: FigureComposer / Plotter / Animator
nodes.fn: Renders profiles, pixel maps, parameter/slot plots, tomogram slices, SSIM curves and walk animations via the plots/* plotter family.

nodes.label: Report / ReportPayloadBuilder
nodes.fn: Assembles the run summary, headline and full metric tables and embedded figures into a markdown inference report.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained backbone weights and x_axis loaded and wrapped for the forward pass.

artifacts.name: normalization_stats.json
artifacts.desc: Per-channel stats and output clamp used to denormalize predictions and GT.

artifacts.name: meta/geometry_field.npz
artifacts.desc: Per-pixel kz field reused to reproject curves for interferometric consistency.

artifacts.name: cubes/*.npy
artifacts.desc: Stitched pred/GT curve cubes, pred/GT parameter cubes and per-pixel metric maps.

artifacts.name: reduced_tomogram_*.npy
artifacts.desc: Classical Capon reconstruction from the trained secondary subset, cached per crop/selection.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: All global metrics including reduced-comparison, data-consistency and track summaries.

artifacts.name: figures/*.png + animations/*.gif
artifacts.desc: Profile, slice, pixel-map, parameter, slot and baseline figures plus per-axis walk animations.

artifacts.name: report.md
artifacts.desc: Assembled inference report with run summary, metric tables and embedded figures.

folders.title: Profile Autoencoder
folders.blurb: Standalone per-pixel profile-autoencoder subsystem that compresses Gaussian-mixture elevation profiles into latent embeddings: it builds per-pixel curve datasets, trains a reconstruction autoencoder whose checkpoint seeds the JEPA target encoder, and runs reconstruction inference with metrics and reports.

diagrams.title: Per-pixel Profile Dataset
diagrams.blurb: Turns the preprocessing Gaussian-parameter volume into per-split, per-pixel intensity-curve DataLoaders, fitting a log1p-standardize normalizer on the training split.

nodes.label: ProfileDatasetConfig
nodes.fn: Holds profile dataset settings: split regions, gaussian axis bounds, pixel subsample and keep-empty fractions, loader params and augmentation config.

nodes.label: Layout
nodes.fn: Resolves the preprocessing run layout and memory-maps the extracted Gaussian-parameter volume plus its global crop and profile length.

nodes.label: ProfileDatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences dataset construction: builds the elevation axis, crops per-split parameters, fits the normalizer on train, then assembles the three DataLoaders.

nodes.label: ParameterCropper
nodes.fn: Slices the memmapped parameter volume into per-split train/val/test region arrays validated against the global crop.

nodes.label: GaussianAxis / GaussianMixture
nodes.fn: Builds the shared elevation axis and evaluates Gaussian-mixture (amp, mu, sigma) parameters into per-pixel intensity curves.

nodes.label: ProfileDataset
nodes.fn: Per-pixel Dataset that stacks amp/mu/sigma channels, selects active vs empty pixels, and on access synthesizes, augments and normalizes one profile curve.

nodes.label: ProfileAugmenter
nodes.fn: Applies train-only amplitude scaling, circular shift, flip and post-normalization Gaussian noise to each curve.

nodes.label: ProfileStatsComputer / ProfileNormalizer
nodes.fn: Fits log1p-then-standardize statistics on sampled train curves, normalizes/denormalizes curves, and persists the stats JSON.

nodes.label: Loader
nodes.fn: Wraps the three ProfileDatasets into seeded, pinned train/val/test DataLoaders.

artifacts.name: parameters.npy
artifacts.fmt: npy (memmap, C x az x rg)
artifacts.desc: Extracted Gaussian-mixture profile parameters (amp/mu/sigma channels) per pixel from the preprocessing run.

artifacts.name: profile_normalization_stats.json
artifacts.desc: log1p transform loc/scale used to normalize curves; reloaded at profile inference and by JEPA to match the AE's input space.

artifacts.name: train/val/test DataLoaders
artifacts.desc: Curve batches plus x_axis and profile length handed to the training stage.

diagrams.title: Reconstruction Training
diagrams.blurb: Trains a per-pixel profile autoencoder to reconstruct intensity curves; the resulting checkpoint is reused by JEPA as the frozen/finetune target encoder producing latent-embedding targets.

nodes.label: train_profile_autoencoder.py
nodes.fn: Thin bootstrap that pins the GPU and hands ProfileAeEntryConfig + SingleTrainRunner to the SeedSweepLauncher.

nodes.label: ProfileAeEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Entry config for a profile-AE run: AE model name, AE loss, seed sweep, pixel subsample, augmentation and training knobs.

nodes.label: SingleTrainRunner
nodes.fn: Per-seed runner (EntryConfigTrainRunner) that instantiates and runs the TrainingPipeline for the selected AE model.

nodes.label: ProfileDatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: Dataset stage that supplies the train/val/test DataLoaders, x_axis and profile length consumed here (see Dataset diagram).

nodes.label: TrainingPipeline (AutoencoderTrainingPipeline)
nodes.fn: Profile-AE training orchestrator built on the shared AutoencoderTrainingPipeline.run scheduler: prepares data, builds the model, saves metadata, runs the overfit gate, then trains.

nodes.label: get_profile_autoencoder
nodes.fn: Builds the selected profile autoencoder (encoder->embedding->decoder) from the registry; reconstruct() returns curve_hat and latent z.

nodes.label: Trainer
nodes.fn: Profile trainer hook: reshapes each batch to (B, L, 1, 1), calls model.reconstruct and scores it with the reconstruction Loss.

nodes.label: Loss (ReconstructionLoss)
nodes.fn: Curve reconstruction loss (huber/charbonnier/curve_kind) comparing reconstructed against input profiles.

nodes.label: AutoencoderTrainer
nodes.fn: Shared AE trainer (extends tools BaseTrainer): builds model_cfg param groups, runs the epoch/validation loop and checkpoints best_model.pt.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.fmt: PyTorch checkpoint (params state_dict, x_axis, epoch, best_val_loss)
artifacts.desc: Trained profile-AE weights; JEPA loads it as the target-provider encoder yielding latent-embedding targets, and profile inference loads it to reconstruct.

artifacts.name: run_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Run summary recording model_name=profile_ae, out_channels=embedding_dim, x_axis_length and seed, validated at load time.

artifacts.name: profile_ae + dataset config JSON
artifacts.desc: Persisted autoencoder architecture config and profile dataset config (ProfileAutoencoderConfigIO + ProfileDatasetConfigIO) used to rebuild the model and dataset downstream.

diagrams.title: Reconstruction Inference
diagrams.blurb: Reloads a trained profile-AE run, reconstructs the chosen split's curves, and emits latent embeddings, reconstruction metrics, figures and a markdown report.

nodes.label: infer_profile_autoencoder.py
nodes.fn: Thin bootstrap that runs InferenceEntry with RunType.PROFILE_AE to launch the profile-AE inference pipeline.

nodes.label: ProfileAeInferenceConfig
nodes.fn: Inference config: run directory, split, device, checkpoint name, batch/worker params and plotting/curve-sampling options.

nodes.label: ProfileAeInferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences inference: loads the run, reconstructs curves, saves embeddings, computes metrics, composes figures and writes the report.

nodes.label: ProfileAeInferenceMetadata (AeInferenceMetadata)
nodes.fn: Shared inference metadata: mints the timestamped output directory and the figures/logs/metrics/report paths.

nodes.label: ProfileAeRunLoader
nodes.fn: Reads run_summary, AE/dataset config and normalization stats, rebuilds the dataset, model and checkpoint into a ProfileAeRun.

nodes.label: ProfileAePredictor (AeReconstructionPredictor)
nodes.fn: Shared reconstruction loop; the profile subclass reshapes curves to (B,L,1,1), runs model.reconstruct, denormalizes to gt/pred and collects embeddings into an AeResult.

nodes.label: ProfileAeMetrics (AeMetricsBase)
nodes.fn: Computes physical, normalized, shape, power/peak and latent-embedding metrics over the reconstructed curves.

nodes.label: ProfileAePlots (AePlotsBase)
nodes.fn: Renders and saves reconstruction overlays, mean profile, error histogram, power scatter and embedding-norm figures.

nodes.label: ProfileAeReport (AeReportBase)
nodes.fn: Assembles the metric tables and figure sections into a markdown inference report.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained profile-AE weights reloaded to reconstruct the split's curves and produce latent embeddings.

artifacts.name: run meta JSON bundle
artifacts.desc: run_summary.json, profile_normalization_stats.json and AE/dataset config used to rebuild the model, normalizer and dataset and validate profile length.

artifacts.name: embeddings.npy
artifacts.desc: Per-curve latent embeddings for the selected split.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Reconstruction, shape, power/peak and embedding metrics for the split.

artifacts.name: report.md + figures
artifacts.desc: Assembled inference report with metric tables and reconstruction/embedding figures.

folders.title: Image Autoencoder
folders.blurb: A 2D convolutional autoencoder that reconstructs multi-channel SAR input patches to learn a latent input embedding; its trained checkpoint feeds JEPA as an image-embedding front-end, while a standalone inference path scores reconstruction quality per run.

diagrams.title: Image-AE DataLoader source
diagrams.blurb: The image autoencoder does not own a dataset layer: BackboneDatasetPreparation runs the backbone DatasetPipeline and hands back input-image patch DataLoaders, of which the AE reads only the input-image channel of each batch.

nodes.label: BackboneDatasetPreparation
nodes.fn: Sets n_gaussians from the gaussian config and builds the GaussianAxis, then runs the backbone DatasetPipeline and returns the train/val/test DataLoaders plus the PatchDataset dict.

nodes.label: DatasetPipeline (backbone)
nodes.fn: External backbone dataset pipeline that crops, patches and normalizes the SAR stack and builds the DataLoaders via shared Loader.build (documented in the backbone map).

nodes.label: train/val/test DataLoaders
nodes.fn: Train/val/test DataLoaders yielding PatchDataset tuples whose first element (batch[0]) is the normalized multi-channel input image the AE reconstructs.

nodes.label: TrainingPipeline (image-AE)
nodes.fn: Image-autoencoder training consumer that reads only batch[0], the input-image patch, from each loader (see the Training diagram).

artifacts.name: meta/stats (normalization)
artifacts.desc: Per-channel input normalization statistics reused to normalize patches in training and to denormalize reconstructions at inference.

artifacts.name: meta/dataset_creation_config.json
artifacts.desc: Serialized dataset/split/patch configuration that the inference loader replays to rebuild the exact split PatchDataset.

diagrams.title: Image-AE training
diagrams.blurb: Trains the image autoencoder to reconstruct its own multi-channel input patches with a reconstruction loss, persisting best_model.pt and the architecture config as the image-embedding front-end later loaded by JEPA.

nodes.label: train_image_autoencoder.py
nodes.fn: Pins the GPU, then hands ImageAeEntryConfig and SingleTrainRunner to SeedSweepLauncher to execute one seed sweep.

nodes.label: ImageAeEntryConfig / ImageAeTrainerConfig
nodes.fn: Entry and trainer config carrying ae_model_name, model_overrides, ae_loss, geometry and dataset paths for the run.

nodes.label: TrainingPipeline (AutoencoderTrainingPipeline)
nodes.fn: Sequences data prep, model build, metadata save, overfit check and training, then returns the run results and directory.

nodes.label: BackboneDatasetPreparation
nodes.fn: External dataset preparation supplying the input-image patch DataLoaders and the derived in_channels (see the Dataset diagram).

nodes.label: get_image_autoencoder / IMAGE_AE_CONFIG_REGISTRY
nodes.fn: Builds the image autoencoder (encoder-decoder exposing reconstruct) from the registry config, wiring in_channels from the dataset.

nodes.label: Loss (ReconstructionLoss)
nodes.fn: Reconstruction loss (mse/huber/charbonnier) between the decoded image and the original input image.

nodes.label: Trainer (AutoencoderTrainer / BaseTrainer)
nodes.fn: Runs the epoch loop reconstructing batch[0] via model.reconstruct, scores it against itself with the criterion, and saves the best checkpoint.

nodes.label: ImageAutoencoderConfigIO + run_summary
nodes.fn: Persists the image-autoencoder architecture config and the run_summary (model_name=image_ae, in/out channels, seed) to the run meta directory.

nodes.label: JepaTrainingPipeline
nodes.fn: Downstream JEPA training that loads best_model.pt and the arch config as a frozen or finetuned image-embedding front-end.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained image-AE weights = latent input-embedding front-end consumed by JEPA and reloaded by inference.

artifacts.name: meta/image_autoencoder config
artifacts.desc: Architecture name, embedding_dim and in_channels of the trained autoencoder.

artifacts.name: meta/run_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Run identity: model_name=image_ae, in/out channels, x_axis_length and seed, checked at inference load.

diagrams.title: Image-AE inference
diagrams.blurb: Reconstructs a trained image-AE run over a chosen split, saving pooled latent embeddings, reconstruction and embedding metrics, diagnostic figures and a markdown report.

nodes.label: infer_image_autoencoder.py
nodes.fn: Routes the IMAGE_AE run type into InferenceEntry, which schedules per-run reconstruction scoring via ImageAeInferenceRunner.

nodes.label: ImageAeInferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences setup, run load, reconstruction, embedding save, metrics, plots and report for one image-AE run.

nodes.label: ImageAeInferenceMetadata (AeInferenceMetadata)
nodes.fn: Creates the output/figures/logs directories under run/inference/image_ae and holds the metrics and report paths.

nodes.label: ImageAeRunLoader (RunLoader)
nodes.fn: Reads run_summary and dataset config, rebuilds the AE model, loads best_model.pt and norm stats, and rebuilds the split PatchDataset and DataLoader into an ImageAeRun.

nodes.label: ImageAePredictor (AeReconstructionPredictor)
nodes.fn: Iterates the loader, reconstructs batch[0], denormalizes gt/pred and pools the latent into a per-patch embedding, yielding an AeResult.

nodes.label: ImageAeMetrics (AeMetricsBase)
nodes.fn: Computes physical, normalized and per-channel reconstruction errors plus latent embedding statistics.

nodes.label: ImageAePlots (AePlotsBase)
nodes.fn: Renders the reconstruction triplets, per-channel MSE bar, error histogram, intensity scatter and embedding-norm histogram (whole plot family).

nodes.label: ImageAeReport (AeReportBase)
nodes.fn: Assembles a markdown report grouping the metric tables and figure sections for the run.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained image-AE checkpoint the loader restores to reconstruct the split.

artifacts.name: embeddings.npy
artifacts.desc: Per-patch pooled latent embeddings for the evaluated split.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Reconstruction (physical/normalized/per-channel) and embedding metrics for the run/split.

artifacts.name: figures/*.png
artifacts.desc: Reconstruction triplets, per-channel MSE bar, error histogram, intensity scatter and embedding-norm plots.

artifacts.name: report.md
artifacts.desc: Assembled inference report embedding the metric tables and figure sections.

folders.title: JEPA
folders.blurb: JEPA trains a spatial backbone to predict a pretrained profile-autoencoder's latent embedding of each pixel's elevation profile (optionally behind an image-autoencoder frontend), then at inference decodes those embeddings back into elevation curves and scores them.

diagrams.title: JEPA Predictor Training
diagrams.blurb: Couples one or two pretrained autoencoders to a spatial backbone and trains that backbone to predict, in latent space, the profile-autoencoder embedding of each pixel's elevation curve.

nodes.label: train_jepa.py
nodes.fn: Pins the GPU environment, builds JepaEntryConfig, and launches SingleTrainRunner through the SeedSweepLauncher.

nodes.label: JepaEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass carrying the backbone name, the profile/image autoencoder runs and freeze-or-finetune modes, target_provider, embedding_loss and param_loss knobs.

nodes.label: TrainingPipeline
nodes.fn: Resolves the autoencoder runs, assembles JepaTrainerConfig, builds the module, runs the overfit gate, and launches the trainer.

nodes.label: Preprocessing arrays
nodes.fn: Preprocessing run supplying the input SLC stack and per-pixel Gaussian-parameter arrays consumed by the dataset preparation.

nodes.label: Profile-AE checkpoint
nodes.fn: Pretrained profile-autoencoder best_model.pt plus its config and ProfileStats, owned by the profile-autoencoder pipeline.

nodes.label: Image-AE checkpoint
nodes.fn: Pretrained image-autoencoder best_model.pt plus its config, owned by the image-autoencoder pipeline, used as an optional spatial frontend.

nodes.label: BackboneDatasetPreparation
nodes.fn: Shared preparation that crops patches and yields train/val/test DataLoaders of (input stack, GT params) plus the elevation x-axis.

nodes.label: JepaModule
nodes.fn: Composes the backbone with the loaded profile and/or image autoencoders into one module whose forward maps a patch to a latent embedding.

nodes.label: CouplingMode / TargetProvider
nodes.fn: Freezes or fine-tunes each autoencoder and supplies the target embedding branch (stopgrad or live) that the backbone is trained to match.

nodes.label: Trainer
nodes.fn: Trains the backbone predictor (optionally fine-tuning the autoencoders) in latent space and updates the EMA target after each optimizer step.

nodes.label: EmbeddingLoss
nodes.fn: Scores predicted vs autoencoder-encoded GT embeddings via MSE/cosine/smooth-L1 plus optional curve reconstruction, falling back to backbone ParameterLoss when no profile AE is present.

nodes.label: JEPA predictor checkpoint
nodes.fn: Best predictor checkpoint holding the backbone plus any coupled autoencoder weights and the x-axis, written by the trainer.

artifacts.name: profile_autoencoder best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Pretrained profile-autoencoder weights (frozen or fine-tuned) loaded into the JEPA module.

artifacts.name: image_autoencoder best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Pretrained image-autoencoder weights used as the optional spatial frontend.

artifacts.name: preprocessing input + parameter arrays
artifacts.desc: Input SLC stack and per-pixel Gaussian-parameter ground truth for the scene.

artifacts.name: JEPA run meta (configs + ProfileStats)
artifacts.desc: Backbone and autoencoder configs, run_summary and profile normalization stats persisted for inference.

artifacts.name: JEPA predictor best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained backbone (plus coupled AE) predictor checkpoint with the elevation x-axis.

diagrams.title: JEPA Curve Inference
diagrams.blurb: Reloads a trained JEPA run, decodes backbone embeddings back into elevation curves across the scene, and scores both curve-space and embedding-space accuracy.

nodes.label: InferenceComponentsResolver
nodes.fn: Inspects the run's meta configs and selects the JEPA (profile) or JEPA-param (image-only) inference component set.

nodes.label: JEPA predictor checkpoint
nodes.fn: Trained JEPA predictor best_model.pt with meta configs and ProfileStats produced by JEPA training.

nodes.label: JEPA_INFERENCE_COMPONENTS
nodes.fn: Immutable binding of loader, predictor, embedding-evaluator classes and the param_space flag for a JEPA run.

nodes.label: InferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Shared orchestrator that loads the run, predicts, evaluates metrics and embeddings, then writes figures and the report.

nodes.label: JepaRunLoader
nodes.fn: Rebuilds JepaModule from meta configs and best_model.pt and wraps it as a curve-predicting adapter (JepaParamRunLoader variant for image-only param runs).

nodes.label: JepaInferenceModel
nodes.fn: Runs backbone -> profile-AE decode -> denormalize, turning an input patch into a physical elevation curve.

nodes.label: JepaCurvePredictor
nodes.fn: Runs the GPU forward pass, stitches predicted and GT curve cubes, and computes per-pixel MSE/MAE/R2/cosine maps.

nodes.label: JepaEmbeddingEvaluator
nodes.fn: Scores embedding MSE/cosine and decoder-only versus full-chain curve MSE between predicted and GT-encoded embeddings.

nodes.label: Curve cubes + pixel maps
nodes.fn: pred_curves and gt_curves cubes with per-pixel metric maps saved as .npy for figures and the report.

nodes.label: metrics.json
nodes.fn: Aggregated pixel-space and JEPA embedding diagnostics written to metrics.json.

artifacts.name: JEPA predictor best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained backbone-plus-autoencoder predictor checkpoint reloaded for inference.

artifacts.name: JEPA run meta (configs + ProfileStats)
artifacts.desc: Backbone/autoencoder configs, run_summary and profile stats used to rebuild the module.

artifacts.name: pred/gt curve cubes + pixel maps
artifacts.desc: Stitched predicted and GT elevation-curve cubes with per-pixel MSE/MAE/R2/cosine/peak maps.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Global pixel-space metrics merged with JEPA embedding and chain diagnostics.

folders.title: Unrolled Physics
folders.blurb: The isolated gamma_net family: LISTA-style unrolled inversion of the exact tomographic forward operator, trained on synthesised per-pixel coherence measurements. Shares only leaf utilities with the rest of the codebase so it can evolve independently.

diagrams.title: Unrolled physics inversion training
diagrams.blurb: Trains the gamma_net unrolled inversion network on per-pixel coherence measurements synthesised from the ground-truth Gaussian profiles through the exact kz steering operator. The pipeline reuses the backbone dataset stack with the geometry field forced on, and the trainer reuses the shared warmup, scheduler, EMA and VRAM-reservation runtime.

nodes.label: UnrolledEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Slim entry dataclass: paths, UnrolledTrainingConfig (schedule, warmup, EMA, VRAM reservation), geometry, normalization, augmentation, curve loss and measurement-noise knobs.

nodes.label: train_unrolled.py
nodes.fn: Pins the GPU environment, then defers to UnrolledTrainingLauncher with ConfigCli overrides.

nodes.label: ConfigFactory
nodes.fn: Duck-typed over the entry config: builds the DatasetConfig with split regions and patch tiling exactly as for backbone training.

nodes.label: UnrolledTrainingLauncher / UnrolledTrainingPipeline
nodes.fn: Resolves the entry config, creates the run directory, builds dataset, model and trainer, and persists the resolved config and model architecture.

nodes.label: get_unrolled / GammaNet
nodes.fn: LISTA-style unroll: matched-filter init, then L iterations of Lipschitz-normalised gradient step, learned 1D prox along elevation, and nonnegative soft-threshold.

nodes.label: UnrolledModelConfigIO
nodes.fn: Persists the GammaNetConfig to unrolled_model_config.json so a checkpoint reconstructs a structurally identical model.

nodes.label: DatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: The backbone dataset stack with build_geometry_field=True: patches carry (input, gt params, per-pixel kz) triples.

nodes.label: TomoOperator
nodes.fn: Exact forward/adjoint pair built per batch from the kz map and elevation axis; reuses PhysicalLoss.synthesise_track for the forward map.

nodes.label: UnrolledTrainer
nodes.fn: Synthesises normalised coherence measurements from GT profiles, optimises the masked curve loss with AdamW, and validates, checkpoints and tests under the EMA weights when enabled.

nodes.label: GaussianCurve
nodes.fn: Renders the denormalised GT Gaussian parameters to power-normalised profiles on the elevation grid; these are both the measurement source and the regression target.

nodes.label: Warmup / Scheduler / WeightEma / VramReservation
nodes.fn: Shared runtime components: linear LR warmup into cosine annealing, per-step EMA shadow updates, and the pre-training VRAM parking guard.

edges.label: warmup step, EMA update, VRAM fill/refill

artifacts.name: best.pt
artifacts.desc: Best-validation weights (the EMA shadow when use_ema is on); reloaded before the final test pass.

artifacts.name: last.pt
artifacts.desc: Final-epoch weights saved after training completes.

artifacts.name: training_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Per-epoch train/val loss and peak-MAE history plus final test metrics.

artifacts.name: unrolled_model_config.json
artifacts.desc: Persisted GammaNetConfig for faithful checkpoint reconstruction.

artifacts.name: docs/resolved_entry_config.json
artifacts.desc: The fully resolved entry configuration the run was launched with.

folders.title: Shared Foundation
folders.blurb: The shared foundation layer every per-model TomoSAR pipeline plugs into: config construction and persistence, dataset and loader scaffolding, model instantiation, seed-sweep training with an overfit gate, typed inference dispatch, and reusable sweep and session orchestrators.

diagrams.title: Config construction & persistence
diagrams.blurb: ConfigFactory turns one run/entry config into the DatasetConfig, BackboneTrainerConfig and InferenceConfig the pipelines consume, while TrainingRunMetadata lays out the run directory and the ConfigIO family persists every config as self-describing meta JSON.

nodes.label: BenchmarkConfig (entry config)
nodes.fn: Holds the paths, training, normalization and inference knobs for one run and feeds the factory.

nodes.label: preprocessing dataset.json
nodes.fn: Preprocessing layout read by the factory for the global crop and Gaussian/geometry resolution.

nodes.label: ConfigFactory
nodes.fn: Builds DatasetConfig, BackboneTrainerConfig and InferenceConfig from the run config plus dataset.json.

nodes.label: TrainingRunMetadata
nodes.fn: Creates the run directory tree and TensorBoard writer and persists trainer_config, run_summary and test_metrics.

nodes.label: ConfigIO family
nodes.fn: Serializes and reloads backbone/profile-AE/image-AE model and dataset configs to and from meta JSON via the config registries.

nodes.label: training pipeline
nodes.fn: Consumes the factory-built dataset and trainer configs to run a per-model training job.

nodes.label: inference resolver
nodes.fn: Reloads the persisted meta configs to reconstruct the architecture at inference time.

artifacts.name: model_config.json
artifacts.desc: Persisted architecture name and fields to faithfully rebuild the backbone net at inference.

artifacts.name: profile_dataset_config.json
artifacts.desc: Split regions and dataset knobs that make a profile-autoencoder run self-describing.

artifacts.name: run_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Channel counts, x-axis length, n_gaussians and seed recorded for the run.

artifacts.name: trainer_config.json
artifacts.desc: Full serialized trainer config saved under the run docs directory.

artifacts.name: test_metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Held-out test metrics used for cross-run comparison.

diagrams.title: Shared dataset & loaders
diagrams.blurb: Shared dataset scaffolding: Layout resolves preprocessing artifacts, BackboneDatasetPreparation drives the backbone DatasetPipeline and derives the Gaussian axis, and Loader builds reproducible train/val/test DataLoaders reused by the backbone, JEPA and image-autoencoder pipelines.

nodes.label: DatasetConfig
nodes.fn: Describes split regions, patch geometry, normalization and loader parameters for the dataset build.

nodes.label: BackboneDatasetPreparation
nodes.fn: Sets n_gaussians, builds the GaussianAxis from profile length, and runs the DatasetPipeline to return loaders, datasets and the x-axis.

nodes.label: Layout
nodes.fn: Loads dataset.json, resolves artifact paths, reads profile_length from the tomogram, and maps secondary labels to indices.

nodes.label: DatasetPipeline
nodes.fn: Builds spatial patch datasets from the resolved artifacts and hands them to the shared Loader.

nodes.label: Loader
nodes.fn: Wraps train/val/test datasets in DataLoaders with seeded workers and reproducible generators.

nodes.label: trainer / model pipeline
nodes.fn: Receives the DataLoaders, x-axis and datasets to train the network.

artifacts.name: dataset.json
artifacts.desc: Preprocessing layout: global crop, artifact filenames and pass labels.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Full-resolution tomogram cube whose first axis sets profile_length.

artifacts.name: parameters
artifacts.desc: Per-pixel Gaussian parameter arrays used as regression targets.

diagrams.title: Model instantiation
diagrams.blurb: ModelBuilder instantiates an architecture config from the model registry and applies per-run overrides, producing the config dataclass every training pipeline uses to build and persist its network.

nodes.label: backbone_name + model_overrides
nodes.fn: Names the architecture and the fields to override for this run.

nodes.label: config registries
nodes.fn: Maps model names to their config-dataclass constructors for backbone, image-AE and profile-AE families.

nodes.label: ModelBuilder
nodes.fn: Instantiates the registry config and validates and applies each model override.

nodes.label: model config dataclass
nodes.fn: The resolved architecture config passed on to build and persist the network.

nodes.label: training pipeline
nodes.fn: Builds the nn.Module from the config and persists it via the config-IO layer.

artifacts.name: model_config.json
artifacts.desc: Persisted architecture name and fields to rebuild the net at inference.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained network weights consumed by inference.

diagrams.title: Seed-sweep training scaffolding
diagrams.blurb: The shared training scaffolding every train_* entry uses: SeedSweepLauncher parses the CLI, SeedSweepRunner fans out over seeds, EntryConfigTrainRunner runs the pretrain preflight then the per-model pipeline, WorkerBase backs the benchmark/CV subprocess workers, and OverfitCheck gates training by memorizing a handful of examples.

nodes.label: train_*.py
nodes.fn: Thin bootstrap that pins the GPU env and hands an entry config plus runner class to SeedSweepLauncher.

nodes.label: SeedSweepLauncher
nodes.fn: Applies ConfigCli to the entry config and forwards it to the seed sweep.

nodes.label: WorkerBase
nodes.fn: Base for the benchmark and cross-validation subprocess workers, fixing the run directory and loss-probe config for one training unit.

nodes.label: SeedSweepRunner + SeedSet
nodes.fn: Deep-copies the config per seed, names each run, and invokes the runner factory for every seed.

nodes.label: EntryConfigTrainRunner
nodes.fn: Runs the pretrain preflight then hands the config to the concrete per-model training pipeline.

nodes.label: PretrainPreflight
nodes.fn: Optionally runs batch-size finding and dataloader tuning through a PretrainOrchestrator before training starts.

nodes.label: per-model training pipeline
nodes.fn: Builds the trainer and dataset then runs the training loop for backbone, JEPA or autoencoder.

nodes.label: OverfitCheck
nodes.fn: Sanitizes the trainer and model config, overfits n examples, writes the verdict, and aborts training on failure.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained checkpoint at the run root handed to inference.

artifacts.name: overfit_report.json
artifacts.desc: Verdict and per-epoch losses proving the sanitized model can memorize n examples.

artifacts.name: pretrain result
artifacts.desc: Batch-size and dataloader tuning results from the preflight probe.

artifacts.name: test_metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Held-out test metrics written at the end of training.

diagrams.title: Typed inference dispatch
diagrams.blurb: InferenceEntry runs either as a scheduler that discovers and type-filters run roots then spawns per-run GPU workers, or as a worker that resolves the typed runner and inference components for one swept run directory.

nodes.label: InferenceEntry
nodes.fn: Dispatches to scheduler or worker mode by --worker and selects the runner/config for the run type.

nodes.label: InferenceScheduler
nodes.fn: Discovers run directories under the roots, keeps only the matching run type, saves the resolved config, and builds one GPU job per run.

nodes.label: RunClassifier
nodes.fn: Classifies a run directory as backbone, profile-AE or image-AE by which meta config file exists.

nodes.label: GpuQueue
nodes.fn: Runs the per-run worker subprocesses across the available GPUs and collects their results.

nodes.label: inference dispatch runners
nodes.fn: Per-type worker that rebuilds the InferenceConfig for one run and launches its typed inference pipeline.

nodes.label: InferenceComponentsResolver
nodes.fn: Chooses backbone vs JEPA spatial inference components by the meta configs present in the run.

nodes.label: InferencePipeline
nodes.fn: Runs sliding-window prediction, stitching and reporting for the resolved run.

artifacts.name: resolved_config.json
artifacts.desc: Resolved inference config serialized for each GPU worker subprocess.

artifacts.name: model_config.json
artifacts.desc: Meta config whose presence routes a run to the right typed runner and components.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Checkpoint loaded to score the run.

diagrams.title: Sweep & session orchestration
diagrams.blurb: Two reusable base orchestrators: StagedPipeline scaffolds multi-stage GPU sweeps with a resumable per-stage state file, and SequentialSessionScheduler runs preprocessing and param-extraction sessions one at a time through a single-worker process pool.

nodes.label: pipeline config
nodes.fn: Supplies the run tag, log base directory and session/stage parameters for the orchestrators.

nodes.label: StagedPipeline
nodes.fn: Sets up the run directory, saves the resolved config, and marks each stage status into state.json.

nodes.label: SequentialSessionScheduler
nodes.fn: Enumerates sessions and dispatches them sequentially, collecting each session's output paths.

nodes.label: BenchmarkPipeline / CrossValidationPipeline
nodes.fn: Concrete multi-stage sweeps that mark stages and spawn per-stage GPU jobs on the shared scaffolding.

nodes.label: PreProcess / analysis schedulers
nodes.fn: Concrete session producers for preprocessing and param-extraction that run one session at a time.

nodes.label: ProcessPoolRunner
nodes.fn: Runs the session callables with a single worker so sessions execute strictly sequentially.

nodes.label: state.json / outputs
nodes.fn: Persisted stage state, resolved config and per-session output paths for resume and downstream use.

artifacts.name: state.json
artifacts.desc: Per-stage status and timestamps tracking a multi-stage sweep.

artifacts.name: resolved_config.json
artifacts.desc: Fully resolved run config replayed by every spawned stage worker.

artifacts.name: session outputs
artifacts.desc: Per-session output paths (e.g. preprocessing dataset, param arrays) fed downstream.

folders.title: Cross-Validation
folders.blurb: K-fold spatial cross-validation subsystem that partitions the scene by azimuth into disjoint train/val/test folds, trains and evaluates one model per fold across GPUs by delegating to the shared training and inference pipelines, and aggregates the per-fold checkpoints and metrics into a cross-validation report.

diagrams.title: K-Fold Cross-Validation Pipeline
diagrams.blurb: Partitions the azimuth extent into azimuth-disjoint folds, schedules per-fold training and inference as GPU subprocesses that delegate to the shared training and inference pipelines, then collects each fold's checkpoint and metrics into mean-and-std cross-validation reports.

nodes.label: cross_validate.py
nodes.fn: Pins the environment and either launches the CV scheduler or, with --worker, runs a single fold's train or infer subprocess.

nodes.label: CrossValidationConfig
nodes.fn: Holds the k-fold run configuration: fold count and azimuth window, seeds, training and inference settings, GPUs, and dataset paths.

nodes.label: CrossValidationPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences fold planning, the training stage, the inference stage, and the report stage, marking each stage's completion status.

nodes.label: FoldConfigFactory
nodes.fn: Builds the azimuth-validated FoldPlanner and derives per-fold trainer, dataset, and inference configs from the CV config.

nodes.label: FoldPlanner
nodes.fn: Partitions the azimuth extent into n_folds guard-trimmed blocks and assigns disjoint train/val/test SplitRegions for each fold.

nodes.label: FoldTrainingStage
nodes.fn: Queues one training GpuJob per fold and seed and schedules them across the available GPUs.

nodes.label: FoldInferenceStage
nodes.fn: Queues one inference GpuJob per fold, seed, and split, skipping missing checkpoints and disjoint splits, and runs them across GPUs.

nodes.label: CrossValidationReportStage
nodes.fn: Collects per-fold results via FoldCollector and drives CrossValidationReport to write the aggregate report.

nodes.label: FoldTrainingWorker
nodes.fn: Runs one fold's training in a GPU subprocess by dispatching to the backbone, JEPA, or profile-autoencoder training pipeline with the fold's split_regions.

nodes.label: FoldInferenceWorker
nodes.fn: Runs one fold's inference for a given split via the backbone inference pipeline using run-resolved inference components.

nodes.label: FoldCollector
nodes.fn: Reads each fold's checkpoint metadata and per-split inference metrics, groups seed replicas, and aggregates their mean and std.

nodes.label: CrossValidationReport
nodes.fn: Computes cross-fold mean-and-std metrics and writes the aggregate markdown report, per-split comparison reports, and the summary JSON.

nodes.label: shared training pipeline
nodes.fn: Trains one fold's model to convergence and saves its checkpoint (backbone/JEPA/AE training pipeline behind WorkerBase).

nodes.label: shared inference pipeline
nodes.fn: Reconstructs the tomogram and evaluation metrics for one fold-split from its checkpoint.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Per-fold trained model weights plus best_val_loss/best_epoch metadata.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Per-fold, per-split inference evaluation metrics.

artifacts.name: training_results.json
artifacts.desc: Per-fold training job status and wall-clock duration.

artifacts.name: cv_aggregate_report.md
artifacts.desc: Cross-fold mean-and-std metric tables plus the fold plan and per-fold training summary.

artifacts.name: cv_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Machine-readable per-split, per-fold aggregate statistics for the CV run.

folders.title: Benchmarking
folders.blurb: The benchmarking subsystem fairly compares backbone architectures under a fixed parameter budget and separately tunes DataLoader throughput, producing leaderboards and recommended run settings.

diagrams.title: Capacity-matched architecture x loss benchmark
diagrams.blurb: Sweeps every backbone architecture, each width-scaled to the reference model's parameter count, across the selected loss components and seeds, then ranks their trained-and-inferred results on a leaderboard against that reference.

nodes.label: benchmark.py
nodes.fn: Bootstraps the run: builds BenchmarkConfig via ConfigCli for the scheduler, or pins one GPU and dispatches a maxbatch/train/infer worker subprocess.

nodes.label: compare_runs.py
nodes.fn: Standalone entry that resolves a benchmark run tag and re-runs ComparisonStage to rebuild the leaderboard from existing training and inference outputs.

nodes.label: BenchmarkConfig
nodes.fn: Benchmark run configuration: model sweep, seeds, sweep_loss_components, and the size_match / max_batch / training / inference / comparison sub-configs.

nodes.label: BenchmarkPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences sweep validation, capacity matching, max-batch probing, queued training then inference, and the final comparison report.

nodes.label: SeedExpandedStage
nodes.fn: Expands the model list into architecture x loss-component x seed run units and builds the per-unit worker CLI commands shared by the training and inference queues.

nodes.label: SizeMatcher
nodes.fn: Binary-searches each model's width scale (WidthScaler rules, DegeneracyAuditor flags) until its parameter count matches the reference model, writing per-model overrides to size_match.json.

nodes.label: MaxBatchProbe
nodes.fn: Runs the real Trainer training step under the swept loss union to find the largest power-of-two batch whose peak VRAM stays under budget (BatchSizeFinder + TrainStepMemoryProbe), writing max_batch.json.

nodes.label: TrainingWorker
nodes.fn: Per-unit subprocess that applies the matched width overrides and measured batch size, then runs the backbone TrainingPipeline for that architecture, loss component and seed.

nodes.label: InferenceWorker
nodes.fn: Per-unit subprocess that runs the backbone InferencePipeline over each trained run directory to produce inference metrics and figures.

nodes.label: BenchmarkSeedCollector + ComparisonReport
nodes.fn: Groups per-seed runs by model, aggregates metrics with dispersion, and hands the TrialRecords to the shared ComparisonReport that ranks every capacity-matched unit against the reference.

artifacts.name: resolved_config.json
artifacts.desc: Resolved BenchmarkConfig persisted so each worker subprocess reloads the exact run settings.

artifacts.name: size_match.json
artifacts.desc: Per-model width overrides, scale and matched parameter count from capacity matching.

artifacts.name: max_batch.json
artifacts.desc: Largest power-of-two batch size and measured peak VRAM per model under the configured budget.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained backbone checkpoint per architecture x loss x seed, written by the backbone TrainingPipeline invoked in the worker.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Per-run inference metrics written by the backbone InferencePipeline for each trained unit.

artifacts.name: benchmark_overview.md
artifacts.desc: Leaderboard ranking every capacity-matched architecture x loss unit against the reference model, with per-seed dispersion.

diagrams.title: DataLoader throughput sweep
diagrams.blurb: Sweeps DataLoader settings (batch size, workers, prefetch, pin-memory) for a chosen model and dataset to find the configuration that keeps the GPU fed; it measures throughput only and never sizes the training batch (that is the benchmark pipeline's max-batch probe).

nodes.label: tune_dataloader.py
nodes.fn: Pins one GPU and single-threads the loader, then runs the DataLoaderTuningPipeline for the configured feed mode.

nodes.label: DataLoaderTuningEntryConfig
nodes.fn: DataLoader tuning configuration: mode, model_name, batch_sizes, worker_counts, prefetch_factors, timed_batches, data_wait_target and the refine flag.

nodes.label: DataLoaderTuningPipeline
nodes.fn: Builds the feed target, constructs the GPU feed benchmark, runs the main and optional refine sweeps, and writes the recommended DataLoader configuration.

nodes.label: FEED_ADAPTERS / build_feed_target
nodes.fn: Builds a FeedTarget (dataset, model, to_model_input, forward_loss) for the selected feed mode - synthetic, profile_autoencoder, image_autoencoder or backbone - reusing the real dataset and model builders.

nodes.label: JsonEventStream
nodes.fn: Streams meta, per-spec result, recommendation and done events over stdout for the web UI to render the sweep live.

nodes.label: DataLoaderSweep
nodes.fn: Runs the benchmark across the LoaderSpec grid (main batch x worker phase, then a prefetch x pin refine phase), emitting each throughput record.

nodes.label: GpuFeedBenchmark
nodes.fn: Times one LoaderSpec end to end, reporting samples/s, data-wait fraction, GPU utilisation and feed ratio over a fixed number of warmup and timed batches.

nodes.label: SweepReport
nodes.fn: Ranks the throughput records, picks the recommended DataLoader configuration respecting the data-wait target, and saves the comparison figures.

artifacts.name: results.json
artifacts.desc: Main and refine sweep records plus the recommended DataLoader config (batch_size, num_workers, prefetch_factor, pin_memory) to apply to real training runs.

artifacts.name: throughput figures
artifacts.desc: One figure per metric (samples/s, data-wait fraction, GPU utilisation, feed ratio) across the swept LoaderSpecs.

artifacts.name: @TUNE event stream
artifacts.desc: Live meta / result / recommendation / done events streamed to the web UI during the sweep.

folders.title: Tune
folders.blurb: An Optuna-based hyperparameter search that distributes short training trials across GPUs, resumes a shared study in chunks, and exports the best configuration per model.

diagrams.title: Optuna Search
diagrams.blurb: Runs a resumable Optuna study per model: the scheduler splits the remaining trial budget across GPU worker subprocesses, each worker samples hyperparameters and trains a short trial reporting to a shared sqlite study, and the best trial is exported as a config alongside diagnostic plots.

nodes.label: tune.py
nodes.fn: Parses CLI flags and either bootstraps the scheduler (default) or, with --worker, pins one GPU and runs a single tuning worker.

nodes.label: TuningEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Dataclass defining the tuning run: training_type, GPU list, skip_models, trial/epoch budget, pruner settings and run paths.

nodes.label: TuningScheduler
nodes.fn: Orchestrates the resumable study per model: fails stale trials, splits the remaining trial budget across GPUs, dispatches workers, then exports best config, plots and a results summary.

nodes.label: GpuQueue
nodes.fn: Runs the per-worker GpuJob commands across the available GPUs and reports each subprocess status back to the scheduler.

nodes.label: TuningWorker
nodes.fn: Per-GPU process that builds the model-family tuner, attaches a TPE sampler and median pruner, loads the shared study, and runs its slice of trials.

nodes.label: Tuner / AeTuner / JepaTuner
nodes.fn: Assembles the search space from the config's tunable lr/arch params and drives study.optimize, running one training pipeline per trial (backbone / profile-AE / image-AE / JEPA variants).

nodes.label: ParamSampler
nodes.fn: Suggests concrete hyperparameters from the space spec through the Optuna trial (float / categorical / indexed_categorical) and decodes them back for reporting.

nodes.label: Trial pipelines/trainers
nodes.fn: Thin subclasses of the real training pipelines/trainers that train one short candidate and report val loss to the trial each epoch, raising TrialPruned when the median pruner says stop.

nodes.label: Optuna Study (sqlite)
nodes.fn: Shared Optuna study persisted in optuna.db that coordinates trial results and TPE suggestions across GPU workers, enabling resume-in-chunks.

nodes.label: BestConfigWriter
nodes.fn: Reads the study's best trial, decodes its params, and writes best_config.json atomically, both as a scheduler step and as a per-trial optimize callback.

nodes.label: StudyPlotter
nodes.fn: Renders the study to publication-style PNGs (optimization history, per-param slice/rank, contours, param/duration importances, EDF, timeline).

artifacts.name: resolved_config.json
artifacts.desc: Fully resolved tuning run config saved by the scheduler and reloaded by each worker subprocess to reconstruct the run.

artifacts.name: optuna.db
artifacts.desc: Shared Optuna study store: workers append trial results and read TPE suggestions, the scheduler reads completed counts to resume in chunks.

artifacts.name: best_config.json
artifacts.desc: Best trial's decoded hyperparameters, exported to configure a full training run of that model.

artifacts.name: tuning_results.json
artifacts.desc: Per-model summary of trial counts, best validation loss and best-config path.

artifacts.name: study_plots/*.png
artifacts.desc: Optuna study diagnostic figures (optimization history, slice/rank, contours, importances, timeline).

folders.title: Comparison
folders.blurb: Three side-by-side comparison pipelines that gather multiple preprocessing, parameter-extraction or trained-model trials and render markdown leaderboards, metric tables and figure galleries.

diagrams.title: Preprocessing Window Comparison
diagrams.blurb: Compares preprocessing runs that differ only in the multilook window size, scoring each full tomogram on the sharpness / speckle / spatial-resolution bias-variance trade-off.

nodes.label: compare_preprocessing_trials.py
nodes.fn: Pins threads, builds the config via ConfigCli, then runs the preprocessing comparison pipeline and logs the report path.

nodes.label: PreprocessingComparisonConfig
nodes.fn: Supplies runs_dir, run_tags, pixel_sample, block_size, range_chunk, workers and make_plots for the window sweep.

nodes.label: Preprocessing runs
nodes.fn: Prior preprocessing runs, each a directory holding a tomogram and its config state.

nodes.label: PreprocessingComparisonPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences collect, threaded measure, optional plot, and report over the window trials.

nodes.label: WindowTrialCollector
nodes.fn: Discovers preprocessing runs and reads each config_state.json to recover the multilook window into a WindowTrial.

nodes.label: WindowMetrics
nodes.fn: Chunks each tomogram's amplitude to measure peak-to-floor contrast, spurious peaks, residual speckle and azimuth correlation length.

nodes.label: SpatialDispersion
nodes.fn: Computes block coefficient-of-variation, block std and FFT autocorrelation length for the speckle and blurring metrics (shared with the param comparison).

nodes.label: WindowComparisonPlots
nodes.fn: Renders one bar chart per window-quality metric across the trials.

nodes.label: MetricTableRenderer
nodes.fn: Builds the per-window markdown metric table, bolding the best value in each column (shared with the param comparison).

nodes.label: WindowComparisonReport
nodes.fn: Assembles the markdown report from the metric table, bar plots and the bias-variance reading guide.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Full complex tomogram whose amplitude drives the window-quality metrics.

artifacts.name: config_state.json
artifacts.desc: Run config; the multilook filter window is read from tomogram_config.filter_arguments.win.

artifacts.name: window_metric_pngs
artifacts.desc: One bar chart per window-quality metric (contrast, speckle CV, spurious peaks, correlation length).

artifacts.name: window_report.md
artifacts.desc: Per-window metric table, comparison plots and the bias-variance reading guide.

diagrams.title: Parameter-Extraction Comparison
diagrams.blurb: Compares Gaussian-fit parameter-extraction runs grouped by the number of Gaussians (K), ranking each K family on complexity-penalised fit, slot collapse and spatial coherence.

nodes.label: compare_param_extraction_trials.py
nodes.fn: Pins threads, builds the config via ConfigCli, then runs the parameter-extraction comparison pipeline.

nodes.label: ParamExtractionComparisonConfig
nodes.fn: Supplies params_dir, run_tags, pixel_sample, block_size and make_plots for the fit comparison.

nodes.label: Param-extraction runs
nodes.fn: Prior Gaussian-fit runs, each holding fitted parameters, meta, and fit diagnostics.

nodes.label: ParamExtractionComparisonPipeline
nodes.fn: Sequences collect, measure, optional plot and report over the K-family trials.

nodes.label: ParamTrialCollector
nodes.fn: Discovers runs marked by param_extraction_meta.json and reads their fit config (k_max, lambda_k, fit flags) into ParamTrials sorted by K.

nodes.label: ParamMetrics
nodes.fn: Loads each trial's parameters, summary and diagnostics to compute R2, BIC, coverage, active/collapsed slots, mu separation and dominant-height coherence.

nodes.label: SpatialDispersion
nodes.fn: Returns the block std of the dominant-scatterer height map used for the spatial-coherence metric (shared with the preprocessing comparison).

nodes.label: ParamComparisonPlots
nodes.fn: Renders K-colour-coded bar charts of BIC, R2, slot collapse, coherence and slot usage.

nodes.label: Leaderboard ranking
nodes.fn: ComparisonReportBase / RankingComputer build the within-K composite score, mean rank and wins from each family's per-trial metrics.

nodes.label: MetricTableRenderer
nodes.fn: Builds the per-trial metric table, bolding the best value within each K family (shared with the preprocessing comparison).

nodes.label: ParamComparisonReport
nodes.fn: Writes the overview (config table plus within-K leaderboards) and the metrics markdown embedding the comparison plots.

artifacts.name: parameters.npy
artifacts.desc: Fitted Gaussian parameters interleaved as amplitude / mu / sigma per slot.

artifacts.name: param_extraction_meta.json
artifacts.desc: Fit config: k_max, lambda_k, sigma_init_divisor, fit flags and activity_threshold.

artifacts.name: fit_metrics_summary.json
artifacts.desc: Global and per-K fit summaries (R2 median, fit coverage, k-ambiguous fraction).

artifacts.name: fit_diagnostics.npz
artifacts.desc: Per-K MSE and best-K maps used for BIC and slot-collapse metrics.

artifacts.name: tomogram_full.npy
artifacts.desc: Read only for its height-bin count, which sets n_data in the BIC computation.

artifacts.name: param_metric_pngs
artifacts.desc: K-colour-coded bar charts of the fit-quality metrics.

artifacts.name: param_overview.md
artifacts.desc: Trial config table plus per-K leaderboards ranked on BIC, R2, coherence and decisiveness.

artifacts.name: param_metrics.md
artifacts.desc: Per-trial metric table (best-in-bold within each K) with the comparison plots.

diagrams.title: Inference Trial Comparison
diagrams.blurb: Collects the trained-model trials of a benchmark and renders a side-by-side report: leaderboards, grouped metric tables and figure/animation galleries across their latest inference runs.

nodes.label: compare_trials.py
nodes.fn: Pins threads, builds the config via ConfigCli, collects the trials and writes the comparison report.

nodes.label: TrialComparisonConfig
nodes.fn: Supplies runs_dir, run_tags, output_dir and the compare_images / compare_gifs / embed_images flags.

nodes.label: Training + inference runs
nodes.fn: Per-trial run directories from training and inference, each holding a checkpoint and a latest inference run.

nodes.label: TrialCollector
nodes.fn: For each run_tag reads the best_model.pt checkpoint and the latest inference run (metrics.json, figures, animations, report.md) into a TrialRecord, extending the shared base collector.

nodes.label: TrialRecord list
nodes.fn: Per-trial container of checkpoint stats, the inference metrics dict, and figure/animation/report paths.

nodes.label: Leaderboard ranking
nodes.fn: ComparisonReportBase / RankingComputer build the magnitude-aware composite score, mean rank, wins and grouped leaderboard from the per-trial metrics.

nodes.label: TrialComparisonReport
nodes.fn: Writes the overview with leaderboards, the grouped metrics comparison, and the per-figure and per-gif side-by-side galleries.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained checkpoint; collector extracts best_val_loss, best_epoch and epoch counts.

artifacts.name: metrics.json
artifacts.desc: Per-trial scalar test metrics from the latest completed inference run.

artifacts.name: inference_media
artifacts.desc: figures/*.png, animations/*.gif and report.md embedded side by side in the comparison.

artifacts.name: overview.md
artifacts.desc: Run overview plus composite, grouped, per-Gaussian precision/recall and count-calibration leaderboards.

artifacts.name: metrics_comparison.md
artifacts.desc: Grouped scalar metric tables with the best value bolded per metric.

artifacts.name: figure_gif_reports
artifacts.desc: Per-figure (figures_*.md) and per-animation (gif_comparison.md) side-by-side pages.

folders.title: Weights X-Ray
folders.blurb: A checkpoint diagnostics tool that scans a runs directory, loads each model's state_dict, and x-rays every parameter tensor for structural pathologies (dead weights, near-uniform layers, rank collapse, dead neurons, non-finite/exploded values, norm-scale collapse and init anomalies), writing console, markdown and JSON reports plus per-tensor plots into each run directory.

diagrams.title: Weights X-Ray
diagrams.blurb: Selects trained backbone checkpoints from a runs directory and, per checkpoint, computes per-tensor weight diagnostics, flags anomalies against configurable thresholds, and emits a console/markdown/JSON report with diagnostic plots into the run directory.

nodes.label: xray_weights
nodes.fn: Pins threads, parses a WeightXrayEntryConfig via ConfigCli, and runs WeightXrayBatch over a runs directory.

nodes.label: WeightXrayEntryConfig
nodes.fn: Holds the runs directory, checkpoint filename, output subdir, plotting flags and threshold knobs, and materializes a per-run WeightXrayConfig with WeightXrayThresholds.

nodes.label: WeightXrayBatch
nodes.fn: Selects run directories then x-rays each one, deriving its per-run WeightXrayConfig and logging the verdict and report path.

nodes.label: RunSelector
nodes.fn: Discovers run directories containing the checkpoint file under the runs dir and filters, prompts, or returns all of them.

nodes.label: WeightXray
nodes.fn: Sequences the per-run resolve, analyze, detect, summarize, plot and report steps for one checkpoint.

nodes.label: StateDictResolver
nodes.fn: Loads the checkpoint with torch.load and locates the parameter tensor mapping inside it (top level or a configured key).

nodes.label: WeightAnalyzer
nodes.fn: Computes per-tensor basic, matrix, spectral (SVD stable/effective rank) and duplicate-unit statistics into a LayerReport for each parameter tensor.

nodes.label: IssueDetector
nodes.fn: Applies threshold rules to each LayerReport, attaching non-finite, dead-weight, uniform, exploded, rank-collapse, dead-neuron, duplicate, init-anomaly, norm-scale-collapse and large-bias issues.

nodes.label: XraySummarizer
nodes.fn: Aggregates the attached issues across tensors into severity counts, issue-code tallies and an overall verdict.

nodes.label: WeightXrayPlots
nodes.fn: Renders per-tensor L2/std/dead-fraction/rank-ratio series, a pooled weight histogram and flagged-layer histograms as PNGs.

nodes.label: WeightXrayReport
nodes.fn: Prints console summary tables and writes the markdown and JSON x-ray reports (embedding the plots) into the run's output directory.

artifacts.name: best_model.pt
artifacts.desc: Trained backbone model checkpoint; its parameter state_dict is the input x-rayed for structural pathologies.

artifacts.name: plots/*.png
artifacts.desc: Per-tensor L2-norm, std, dead-fraction and rank-ratio series plus a pooled weight histogram and flagged-layer histograms.

artifacts.name: weight_xray.md
artifacts.desc: Human-readable x-ray report with summary, issue-by-type table, findings, embedded plots and per-tensor metrics, written into the run dir.

artifacts.name: weight_xray.json
artifacts.desc: Machine-readable x-ray: the summary plus every LayerReport with its computed diagnostics and worst-case severity.
